subject: PluginTransport implementation — cross-package IPC abstraction for desktop plugins

typecheck.desktop_package: pass — 0 errors in validated files; all 40+ errors are pre-existing in other packages (app/ tests, test-utils)

checks.detail: All 5 methods defined with correct signatures
on(channel: string, handler: (data: unknown) => void): () => void
off(channel: string, handler: (data: unknown) => void): void
States 'channel names are automatically namespaced by the host'
All signatures one-to-one compatible with interface
Wraps handler to filter on payload.channel, stores in WeakMap, returns unsubscribe
Retrieves wrapped handler from WeakMap, removes listener

issues.description: WeakMap collision when same handler object is registered on multiple channels
issues.detail: pluginWrapped maps handler → wrapper. If pluginOn('ch1', fn) and pluginOn('ch2', fn) are called with the same fn, the second overwrites the first wrapper. pluginOff would then remove the wrong listener. The returned unsubscribe from the first call still works (closes over original wrapper).
issues.recommendation: Change to Map<string, Map<handler, wrapper>> keyed by channel, or document that handler identity must be unique per channel. Low risk in practice since createElectronPluginTransport creates per-plugin handlers.

recommendations: Consider making pluginOff accept channel parameter for additional filtering in the WeakMap lookup

checks.detail: Accesses api.pluginSend, api.pluginOn, api.pluginOff, api.pluginInvoke via (window as any).api

checks.check: Channel names prefixed with plugin name
checks.detail: prefix = `${pluginName}:`, applied to all channel strings
on tracks subscriptions, off delegates, destroy iterates and clears
transport.destroy() pushed to disposeCallbacks in plugin-loader.ts
createNoopTransport returns silent pass-through for all methods
createPluginTransport detects Electron bridge, falls back to noop

recommendations: Consider declaring a global type augmentation for window.api instead of (window as any)

checks.detail: Uses 'opencode:plugin:*' namespace — distinct from existing channels
IpcHandle/Send/Push derive from typeof IPC which includes new channels
Same export signature as ipc-store.ts, ipc-init.ts etc.
Consistent with existing handler registration patterns

issues.description: handlePluginInvoke calls only first registered handler for RPC
issues.detail: Invoke handlers are stored in a Set but execution uses handlers.values().next().value — only the first handler is called. Multiple handlers for the same RPC channel are silently ignored.
issues.recommendation: This is likely intentional (RPC should have one responder). Document this explicitly so future developers don't assume multi-handler RPC support.

recommendations: Add JSDoc to handlePluginInvoke clarifying only first handler is invoked
Consider throwing if multiple handlers are registered for the same invoke channel (would catch misconfiguration early)

checks.detail: Registered in correct sequence alongside all other handler modules

cross_cutting_concerns.description: No test coverage for the plugin transport system
cross_cutting_concerns.detail: No .test.ts files exist for plugin-transport.ts, plugin-transport-ipc.ts, or the preload transport methods.
cross_cutting_concerns.recommendation: Add unit tests for: createPluginTransport factory, createElectronPluginTransport methods, createNoopTransport, preload bridge methods (pluginSend, pluginOn, pluginOff, pluginInvoke), and main process handler registration

cross_cutting_concerns.description: pluginWrapped WeakMap uses handler identity as sole key — no channel awareness
cross_cutting_concerns.detail: pluginOn stores pluginWrapped.set(handler, wrappedHandler). If same handler object is reused across channels (unlikely in practice since createElectronPluginTransport creates per-plugin closures), the second registration overwrites the first.
cross_cutting_concerns.recommendation: Change to Map<string, WeakMap<Function, Function>> keyed by channel for correct multi-channel cleanup

cross_cutting_concerns.description: pluginOff channel parameter is unused in preload implementation
cross_cutting_concerns.detail: The channel argument passed to pluginOff is ignored because cleanup is driven by handler identity via WeakMap. The channel parameter is part of the PluginTransport interface contract but has no effect at the preload bridge level.
cross_cutting_concerns.recommendation: Either use channel in pluginOff for additional filtering, or add a comment explaining channel is for interface conformance

summary: All 8 files are structurally sound, type-safe, and follow existing patterns. The PluginTransport interface correctly defines send/invoke/on/off/destroy. ElectronPluginTransport uses window.api.* (not ipcRenderer directly). Channel names are prefixed with plugin name. Transport is cleaned up on plugin dispose. Main process handlers follow the established register*IpcHandlers() pattern. IPC channel names follow 'opencode:*' convention. Three minor concerns identified: (1) WeakMap collision risk in preload when same handler registered on multiple channels, (2) No test coverage, (3) handlePluginInvoke only uses first RPC handler. None are blocking.
